zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh.lifeSummary: 當Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo於1203年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，他的父親Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara26歲，母親Elvira Bernardo  Felipa23歲。他於大約1228年與Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1256年在西班牙享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo于1203年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，他的父亲Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara26岁，母亲Elvira Bernardo  Felipa23岁。他于大约1228年与Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1256年在西班牙享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pérez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .
Breton (Pérez): variant of Pérès (see Peres ).

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
Czech (Pařez): nickname from pařez ‘(tree) stump’.
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: Suárez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Jesus, Mario, Roberto, Pedro, Raul.
Spanish (Suárez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Suero .

variants.name: Suares
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Suarez  .

variants.name: Suero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Fernando, Ramon, Ana, Jesus, Jorge, Julio, Luis, Orlando, Rafael.
Spanish: from the medieval personal name Suero, from Latin Suarius, from suarius ‘swineherd’, a typical nomen humilitatis (‘name as a sign of humility’) of the early Christian times.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Fernán
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Hernán
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

variants.name: Hernando
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

en.lifeSummary: When Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo was born in 1203, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, his father, Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara, was 26 and his mother, Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, was 23. He married Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo about 1228, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1256, in Spain, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo est né en 1203, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo environ 1228, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1256, à Espagne, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo 1203 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara 26 und seine Mutter Elvira Bernardo  Felipa 23. Er hat um 1228 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1256 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo è nato il nel 1203 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, il suo padre, Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo circa 1228, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1256, nel Spagna, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledoが1203年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで生まれた時，父Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Laraは26歳，母Elvira Bernardo  Felipaは23歳であった。約1228年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでDoña Luna Estevanes de Toledoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1256年にスペインで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo(이)가 1203년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elvira Bernardo  Felipa(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1228년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1256년 스페인에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo nasceu em 1203, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo aproximadamente 1228, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1256, em Espanha, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo родился в 1203 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, его отцу по имени Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo около 1228 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1256 г. в местности Испания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Fernán Pérez  Suárez de Toledo nació en 1203, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Pedro Perez Ruiz de Guzman Gutierrez Rodriquez - Senor de Lara, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elvira Bernardo  Felipa, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Doña Luna Estevanes de Toledo aproximadamente 1228, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1256, en España, a la edad de 53 años.
